MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Barry D. Hovis, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Curt Simon, and for plaintiff Digi-Shark.
Plaintiff Curt Simon was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Ken Belenger, a/k/a Ken Belanger.
Default was entered against defendant on December 15, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, March 4, 2010.

Exhibit 3---One page document re: Digi-Shark, undated--------------------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 2---One page document re: ShapeSearch, undated-------------------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 2---One page document re: ShapeSearch, undated-------------------------------ID/EVID.

After hearing the evidence presented, good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiffs Digi-Shark and Curt Simon, and against the Defaulted Defendant Ken Belenger, a/k/a Ken Belanger as follows:

(1) Defendant Ken Belenger, a/k/a Ken Belanger shall, within ten business days of service of this judgment, render an accounting and pay plaintiff 50% of the proceeds received from any sale of the search by image product marketed by Digi-Shark;
(2) Defendant is enjoined from continuing to sell the image-based digital search product under the name ShapeSearch, Digi-Shark or any other name until 30 days after defendant has delivered each of the items described in paragraph 2 of the judgment to plaintiff and
(3) Defendant is further enjoined from taking any action that would deprive plaintiff Curt Simon of the abililty to sell the image-based product, including, but not limited to, the sale of the product to a third party for exclusive use.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
